citations.claim: BlueSun denied the prior authorization for semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection.
citations.verbatim_quote: your prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection has been DENIED

citations.claim: The stated basis for denial was failure to meet all GLP-1 criteria.
citations.verbatim_quote: Member does not meet all criteria for GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy

paragraphs.heading: Patient and Requested Service
paragraphs.text: I am writing on behalf of my patient, Jane A. Doe (member ID BS-A1234567, date of birth May 14, 1978), enrolled in BlueSun Health Premium HMO, to formally appeal BlueSun's April 1, 2026 determination that her prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection has been denied. The denial letter states that the Member does not meet all criteria for GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy. The medical record demonstrates that Ms. Doe in fact satisfies each criterion under policy MEDPOL-GLP1-v3, as detailed below.

citations.claim: Policy requires BMI at or above 30 kg/m^2.
citations.verbatim_quote: Body Mass Index (BMI) at or above 30 kg/m^2

citations.claim: National guidelines support pharmacotherapy at this BMI category when lifestyle measures alone are insufficient.
citations.verbatim_quote: Adults with a BMI of 30 kg/m2 or greater, or a BMI of 27 kg/m2 or greater with weight-related comorbidities such as hypertension or type 2 diabetes mellitus, should be offered comprehensive lifestyle interventions, and pharmacotherapy is an appropriate adjunct in these BMI categories when lifestyle measures alone do not achieve sufficient weight loss.

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: BMI at or above 30 kg/m^2
paragraphs.text: MEDPOL-GLP1-v3 requires a Body Mass Index (BMI) at or above 30 kg/m^2. Ms. Doe's most recent measured BMI, documented in her February 15, 2026 progress note, is 34.2 — comfortably above the policy threshold. This is also consistent with the 2013 AHA/ACC/TOS Guideline for the Management of Overweight and Obesity in Adults, which recognizes adults with a BMI of 30 kg/m2 or greater as appropriate candidates for pharmacotherapy when lifestyle measures alone do not achieve sufficient weight loss.

citations.claim: Policy requires at least six months of a supervised diet, exercise, and behavior modification program.
citations.verbatim_quote: supervised program of reduced-calorie diet, increased physical activity, and behavior modification for a minimum of six months

citations.claim: Patient has documented sustained participation in a supervised Weight Watchers program exceeding six months.
citations.verbatim_quote: Patient has attended 21 of 24 scheduled Weight Watchers group sessions

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Supervised diet and exercise for at least six months
paragraphs.text: MEDPOL-GLP1-v3 also requires documented participation in a supervised program of reduced-calorie diet, increased physical activity, and behavior modification for a minimum of six months. Ms. Doe has been enrolled in a structured Weight Watchers program and, as documented in her November 20, 2025 progress note, has attended 21 of 24 scheduled Weight Watchers group sessions — well in excess of the six-month minimum. Despite this sustained, supervised lifestyle effort, she has not achieved a clinically adequate weight reduction, which is precisely the circumstance in which adjunctive pharmacotherapy is indicated.

paragraphs.heading: Conclusion and Request
paragraphs.text: Ms. Doe meets every criterion enumerated in MEDPOL-GLP1-v3 for GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy: her BMI of 34.2 exceeds the 30 kg/m^2 threshold, and she has completed well over six months of documented, supervised lifestyle intervention without achieving sufficient weight loss. Her clinical situation aligns with national obesity-management guidelines recommending pharmacotherapy as an appropriate adjunct in this setting. I respectfully request that BlueSun Health Premium HMO overturn the April 1, 2026 denial and approve coverage of semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection. Thank you for your prompt reconsideration.

input.subject_line: Appeal of prior authorization denial — semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection — member BS-A1234567

citations.claim: The prior authorization for semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly was denied.
citations.verbatim_quote: your prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection has been DENIED

citations.claim: The stated basis for denial was that the member did not meet all criteria for GLP-1 therapy.
citations.verbatim_quote: Member does not meet all criteria for GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy

paragraphs.heading: Patient and Requested Service
paragraphs.text: I am writing on behalf of my patient, Jane A. Doe (Member ID: BS-A1234567; DOB: May 14, 1978), enrolled in BlueSun Health Premium HMO, to formally appeal the April 1, 2026 denial of her prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection. The denial letter states that the request has been denied on the grounds that the member does not meet all criteria for GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy. As outlined below, the medical record demonstrates that Ms. Doe in fact satisfies each applicable criterion under policy MEDPOL-GLP1-v3, and I respectfully request that the denial be overturned.

citations.claim: The policy requires a BMI at or above 30 kg/m^2.
citations.verbatim_quote: Body Mass Index (BMI) at or above 30 kg/m^2

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: BMI at or above 30 kg/m^2
paragraphs.text: Policy MEDPOL-GLP1-v3 requires a Body Mass Index (BMI) at or above 30 kg/m^2. Ms. Doe's documented BMI is 34.2, as recorded in the 2026-02-15 progress note, which comfortably exceeds the policy threshold. This is also consistent with the American Diabetes Association 2024 Standards of Care, which recommend pharmacologic weight-management therapy for adults with BMI ≥ 30.

citations.claim: The policy requires at least six months of supervised diet, exercise, and behavior modification.
citations.verbatim_quote: supervised program of reduced-calorie diet, increased physical activity, and behavior modification for a minimum of six months

citations.claim: The patient's documented participation in supervised Weight Watchers sessions exceeds six months.
citations.verbatim_quote: Patient has attended 21 of 24 scheduled Weight Watchers group sessions

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Six months of supervised diet, exercise, and behavior modification
paragraphs.text: The policy further requires participation in a supervised program of reduced-calorie diet, increased physical activity, and behavior modification for a minimum of six months. Ms. Doe has exceeded this requirement through her sustained participation in a supervised Weight Watchers program: per the 2025-11-20 progress note, she had attended 21 of 24 scheduled Weight Watchers group sessions, reflecting approximately ten months of consistent supervised engagement in diet, activity, and behavior-modification counseling well beyond the six-month minimum.

paragraphs.heading: Conclusion and Request
paragraphs.text: Ms. Doe meets every applicable medical-necessity criterion under MEDPOL-GLP1-v3: her BMI of 34.2 exceeds the 30 kg/m^2 threshold, and she has completed well over six months of supervised diet, exercise, and behavior-modification therapy through Weight Watchers. The stated rationale for denial is therefore not supported by the record. I respectfully request that BlueSun Health Premium HMO overturn the denial and approve coverage of semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection without further delay. Thank you for your prompt reconsideration of this appeal.

input.subject_line: Appeal of prior authorization denial — semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection — member BS-A1234567
